# Implementation Plan: TW-0004
<!-- Generated by /plan -->

**Title:** Close TW-0003 audit gaps (benchmarks honesty + low fixes)
**Spec ID:** TW-0004
**Spec:** docs/specs/spec-TW-0004.md
**Plan size:** Standard

## Task

Close the eight findings (A1–A8) in `docs/audits/spec-TW-0003-validation-2026-07.md`. The reparse tightening code is real and proven; the gap is that the published benchmark docs were never re-measured after it landed, leaving the repo self-contradictory ("shipped" in FEATURES.md vs "not yet done" in BENCHMARKS.md) plus one wall-clock overclaim, one undocumented boundary (block-0 edits), three doc nits, and one latent code footgun. Per the triage assumptions this is a **docs-honesty + small-hardening** pass: correct the claims to measured reality; do not attempt to make top-of-doc edits faster (roadmap).

## Approach

All code/doc-in-repo changes land on `ai/tw-0002` in `.worktrees/TW-0002` (continuation, one review — same convention as TW-0003). The audit doc, spec, and ledger live in the main tree. Order: code fixes first (A8 + tests pinning A3), then re-run the bench producer (`bench/cold-parse.bench.ts`) to get real post-tightening numbers, then rewrite the two docs from those numbers, then close the audit register.

## Reference implementation

- Numbers producer: `bench/cold-parse.bench.ts` (workloads @mid/@end over `bench/fixtures/{small,medium,large}.md`; prints `parseIncrementalWithStats` reuse lines). Its own header comment (`:9-24`) still describes pre-tightening reuse — update it in step 4.
- Doc format: the existing `docs/BENCHMARKS.md` §2 tables/verdict shape and §5's environment-block style; keep min-floor-plus-GC-caveat presentation.
- The audit doc's per-issue "Resolution shape" lines are the checklist: `docs/audits/spec-TW-0003-validation-2026-07.md`.

## Steps

### 1. A8 — clone the suffix on `delta === 0`
- **File:** `src/core/parser.ts` (~1479, in `tryReuseSuffix`)
- **Action:** Modify
- **Details:** `const shifted = delta === 0 ? rest : rest.map(...)` aliases nodes between the old and new trees. Clone in both arms (e.g. always `rest.map((b) => shiftBlock(b, delta))` — `shiftBlock` already `structuredClone`s; a zero shift is a pure clone). If the clone cost @same-length-edit measurably regresses the bench, instead keep the alias but add a test + comment pinning the immutability invariant — prefer the clone.
- **Verify:** new unit test in `src/core/parser.incremental.test.ts`: same-length replacement → assert no reused suffix block is reference-identical (`===`) to a node in `prev`; property oracle (777×2 cases) stays green.

### 2. A3 — pin the block-0 boundary with a test
- **File:** `src/core/parser.incremental.test.ts`
- **Action:** Modify
- **Details:** Add a test documenting the known boundary: an edit inside **block 0** of the 160-line `bigDoc` falls back to full parse (stats show `reparsedFromLine === 0`/full span, `reusedSuffixBlocks === 0`). Title it as a documented boundary, not a bug (e.g. "block-0 edits fall back to full parse (documented boundary — see BENCHMARKS §2)"). No parser change (triage assumption: document, don't engineer away).
- **Verify:** test green; asserts real `parseIncrementalWithStats`.

### 3. A6 — soften the "byte-for-byte" comment
- **File:** `src/react/TypewrightEditor.tsx` (~1303-1305)
- **Action:** Modify
- **Details:** Reword to "behaviourally unchanged (asserted by `e2e/caret-reveal.spec.ts` default-mode test)" — the block branch was touched by TW-0003's a11y fixes, so "byte-for-byte" is false at file scope.

### 4. Re-measure — run the producer on the branch
- **File:** none (measurement step) + `bench/cold-parse.bench.ts` header comment
- **Action:** Run + Modify
- **Details:** In `.worktrees/TW-0002`: `pnpm bench` (quiet machine, note Node/vitest/tinybench versions actually installed — fixes A7's provenance class of error at the source). Capture per fixture: full-parse floor, `parseIncremental` @mid/@end min + mean/p75, and the reuse stats lines (expect 1 MB @mid ≈ `reparsed 9,168→9,170`, ~3,056 suffix blocks reused). Also capture the @top-of-doc case if adding it as a workload is cheap (a `topChange` at ~5% mirroring `midChange` in the fixture setup, `cold-parse.bench.ts:60-70`) — it is the honest number for the A2 caveat; the audit's probe figures (182 ms vs 268 ms full, tsx-inflated) must not be published as-is. Update the file's header honesty note (`:18-24`) to describe the bounded reparse + suffix re-offset.
- **Verify:** bench completes; stats lines show the bounded span (not `→18,317` @mid).

### 5. A1 + A2 + A7 — rewrite `docs/BENCHMARKS.md` §2
- **File:** `docs/BENCHMARKS.md` (worktree copy)
- **Action:** Modify
- **Details:** Replace §2's table, reuse-stats paragraph, and "vs SPEC targets" verdict rows with step-4 numbers. Rewrite the "1 MB miss, stated plainly" narrative: the reparse **span** is now bounded to the dirty block(s) (shipped, `tryReuseSuffix`); state the two honest boundaries — (a) total wall-clock for early-doc edits still scales with the tail (suffix re-offset is O(remaining blocks); publish the measured @top/@5% ratio), (b) an edit inside the very first block falls back to a full parse. Delete "Future work … Tracked, not yet done" item 3 (or convert to "remaining roadmap: make the re-offset lazy to bound wall-clock for early-doc edits; support block-0 cuts"). Re-issue the SPEC-target verdict honestly from the new numbers (if 1 MB @mid parse floor now fits <8 ms, say so; if not, keep the Missed verdict with the new figure). Fix the environment block to the versions step 4 actually printed (A7: vitest is 3.2.6, not 3.2.7).
- **Verify:** `grep -n "not yet done\|reparses from the edit to end-of-document\|9,168→18,317" docs/BENCHMARKS.md` → no hits; §1/§5 Typewright numbers still consistent with §2's full-parse floors.

### 6. A1 + A2 + A4 + A5 — align `docs/FEATURES.md`
- **File:** `docs/FEATURES.md` (worktree copy)
- **Action:** Modify
- **Details:** Four surgical edits: (1) §10 bullet (~:120): replace "currently reparses to end-of-document (~34 ms floor …) … tracked roadmap work" with the shipped-tightening summary + step-4 @mid figure + the two boundaries from step 5. (2) Progress item 2 (~:240): reword "a top-of-doc edit reparses O(edited block), not the whole tail" → "the reparse **span** is O(edited block); total wall-clock for early-doc edits still scales with the re-offset suffix (measured Nx @5% on 1 MB)". (3) Progress item 4 (~:242): append the caveat clause "… zero serious/critical violations (colour-contrast excluded as host-theme scope, documented in the suite)". (4) Caret-reveal coverage note (§ where `hiddenMarkers`/marker coverage is described and/or the Progress boundaries paragraph): note fence markers are computed and tested in core but not yet surfaced in the caret view (code blocks aren't caret-eligible).
- **Verify:** `grep -n "O(edited block), not the whole tail\|tracked roadmap work" docs/FEATURES.md` → no stale hits; §10 and Progress no longer contradict.

### 7. Close the audit register
- **File:** `docs/audits/spec-TW-0003-validation-2026-07.md` (main tree)
- **Action:** Modify
- **Details:** Flip A1–A8 register rows to RESOLVED (A2/A3/A5 as **resolved-scoped**: claims corrected/boundaries documented; the deeper perf/feature work — lazy re-offset, block-0 cuts, editable fences — is roadmap, not a provenance gap). Append a dated changelog entry naming the fix commit(s) and the re-measured headline numbers.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `docs/BENCHMARKS.md` §2 numbers + reuse stats are reproducible from `pnpm bench` on the branch (bounded span visible, e.g. 1 MB @mid `9,168→9,170`), the "not yet done"/reparse-to-EOF text is gone, and the environment block matches the installed versions.
- [ ] `docs/FEATURES.md` §10 and Progress items 2/4 agree with BENCHMARKS.md and each other; the O(edited block) claim is span-scoped with the measured early-doc wall-clock ratio; colour-contrast caveat and fence-marker boundary are present.
- [ ] New unit tests green: same-length edit produces no reference-shared suffix nodes; block-0 edit boundary pinned; property oracle (opts on/off) still deep-equal.
- [ ] Full gates on the branch: `pnpm typecheck`, `pnpm vitest run --exclude '**/parser.perf.test.ts'` (334+), `pnpm e2e` (58), `pnpm build`, `pnpm size` — all green.
- [ ] Audit register A1–A8 = RESOLVED/resolved-scoped with changelog entry.

## Verify

- Run the step-5/6 greps; read §2 + FEATURES §10 side-by-side for agreement.
- `pnpm bench` output vs published table (same machine, min floors within noise).
- Gates per acceptance criteria; commit(s) on `ai/tw-0002`, no push.

## Out of scope

- Making top-of-doc/block-0 edits faster (lazy suffix re-offset, cut=0 support) — documented roadmap, per triage assumption.
- Enabling caret reveal inside fenced code blocks (real feature with new risks).
- Re-running or altering the CM6 §5 comparison (already reproduced by the audit).
